message:
feat(btrfs): resolve named subvolume and snapshot mount selection (bd-sv1b2)

Land the core infrastructure for mounting a specific btrfs subvolume
or snapshot by name (advancing bd-sv1b2). The mount flavor is now
driven by a `BtrfsMountSelection` enum that the open path consults
before handing OpenFs a subvol-rooted BtrfsContext, and the ffs-cli
mount command now threads `--subvol` / `--snapshot` through the
selection parser instead of the previous "not yet supported" stub.

crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:
- New `BtrfsMountSelection` enum with `DefaultRoot` (FS_TREE, objectid
  5), `Subvolume(String)`, and `Snapshot(String)` variants. `Default`
  points at `DefaultRoot` so existing callers default to today's
  behavior, and the `.kind()` helper exposes a stable
  "default_root"/"subvolume"/"snapshot" tag for log lines and error
  formatting.
- `OpenOptions` grows a `btrfs_mount_selection` field (default:
  `DefaultRoot`), so callers can request a specific subvol at open
  time without touching the FS_TREE path.
- Import `enumerate_snapshots` and `enumerate_subvolumes` from
  ffs-btrfs and wire them into the btrfs open path so we can resolve
  the requested name against the live subvol catalog, select the
  matching `BtrfsContext.subvol_root_dirid`, and return a well-formed
  `FfsError` if the name isn't found instead of silently falling
  back to FS_TREE.

crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs:
- Import `BtrfsMountSelection` and drop the "not yet supported"
  clap helper note on `--subvol` / `--snapshot`.
- `MountCmdOptions` grows a `btrfs_mount_selection` field and loses
  `Copy` (now that it holds a `String`). The mount dispatch passes
  through `parse_btrfs_mount_selection(subvol, snapshot)?` instead
  of the former `validate_btrfs_mount_selection(...)?` reject-all
  path, so the CLI flags actually flow into the core selection enum.

crates/ffs-extent/src/lib.rs, crates/ffs-inode/src/lib.rs,
crates/ffs-core/tests/metamorphic.rs:
- Propagate the recent `FsGeometry::first_meta_bg` field addition
  into three test fixtures (`first_meta_bg: 0`) so the extent, inode,
  and metamorphic test harnesses keep compiling against the expanded
  struct without changing their semantics.

.beads/issues.jsonl:
- bd-sv1b2 (btrfs core: resolve named subvolume and snapshot mount
  selections) moved from open to in_progress as this commit delivers
  its core behavior.
- bd-sv2c3 (ffs-cli: wire btrfs --subvol and --snapshot mount flags
  to OpenOptions) opened as the follow-up CLI ergonomics bead (the
  plumbing here already bridges it).
- bd-sv3d4 (add operator-facing coverage and parity evidence for
  btrfs subvolume/snapshot mounts) opened so the docs/parity
  evidence work is tracked.
- bd-mexte (add mounted-path E2E and log-contract coverage for ext4
  EXT4_IOC_MOVE_EXT) opened as follow-up to the earlier MOVE_EXT
  donor-validation landing.
- bd-mmplg (add CLI and runtime log-contract coverage for ext4 MMP
  refusal states) opened to track the remaining MMP observability
  gap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-zvl5z","title":"Freeze exact FfsError string-variant debug contract","description":"Tighten the remaining substring-only ffs-error debug-format test for string variants to an exact golden contract, then validate through rch.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T05:47:35.043413450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T05:53:48.363131060Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T05:53:48.360789138Z","close_reason":"Froze the remaining substring-only ffs-error string-variant debug test to an exact golden contract and validated with rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ffs-error","golden-artifacts","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-zzikz","title":"port ext4 inode i_version rename parity","notes":"Kernel ext4 rename bumps ctime/i_version, not i_generation; this bead tracks the actual rename change-cookie contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T13:27:20.689706867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T13:37:00.444549721Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T13:37:00.441642658Z","close_reason":"Added ext4 rename i_version parity regression and clarified generation preservation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-sv1b2","title":"btrfs core: resolve named subvolume and snapshot mount selections","description":"Survey finding: `FEATURE_PARITY.md` and `README.md` both claim btrfs subvolume mount support, but `OpenFs::from_device` still hardcodes `BTRFS_FS_TREE_OBJECTID` and the operator-facing CLI rejects `--subvol/--snapshot`. This bead owns the core selection logic in `ffs-core`.

Scope:
- Extend `OpenOptions` with an explicit btrfs mount selection contract (default root, named subvolume, named snapshot, and optionally numeric objectid if that simplifies diagnostics).
- Walk the root tree and resolve the requested selection to the mounted `subvol_objectid` plus `subvol_root_dirid`.
- Return deterministic `FfsError` variants for missing selections, ambiguous names, and selection/type mismatches.
- Keep default-root behavior unchanged when no selection is requested.

Acceptance:
- `OpenFs::open_with_options` can mount a non-default subvolume/snapshot without post-open mutation hacks.
- Synthetic and/or kernel-reference tests prove correct resolution for default root, named subvolume, named snapshot, and missing target cases.
- Structured diagnostics include the requested selector and resolved objectid/root dirid.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","mount","parity"]}
+{"id":"bd-sv2c3","title":"ffs-cli: wire btrfs --subvol and --snapshot mount flags to OpenOptions","description":"Survey finding: `ffs-cli` advertises `--subvol`/`--snapshot` on `mount`, but `validate_btrfs_mount_selection()` hard-rejects both flags even though the underlying codebase already has internal btrfs subvolume context machinery. This bead owns the operator-facing CLI plumbing.

Scope:
- Remove the unconditional rejection path in `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs`.
- Thread the requested selection into `OpenOptions` and preserve all existing ext4 behavior.
- Emit structured startup/rejection logs that show requested selector, resolved selection type, and failure class.
- Replace the current `mount_rejects_subvol_flag` / `mount_rejects_snapshot_flag` tests with positive routing tests and deterministic failure diagnostics for nonexistent selectors.

Acceptance:
- `ffs mount ... --subvol <name>` and `ffs mount ... --snapshot <name>` reach `OpenFs::open_with_options` instead of bailing in argument validation.
- Missing selectors fail with clear, stable user-facing errors.
- CLI tests cover default-root, explicit subvolume, explicit snapshot, and nonexistent selection cases.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","cli","mount"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sv2c3","depends_on_id":"bd-sv1b2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-sv3d4","title":"add operator-facing coverage and parity evidence for btrfs subvolume/snapshot mount","description":"Survey finding: current conformance only proves the default btrfs root-alias path, while docs/parity wording imply user-selectable subvolume mount support. This bead closes that evidence gap after the core and CLI work land.

Scope:
- Add harness and/or FUSE E2E coverage that mounts a non-default btrfs subvolume and snapshot through the real CLI/operator path.
- Assert mounted-root `getattr`/`lookup`/`readdir`/`read` behavior is scoped to the selected tree, not the default root.
- Update `FEATURE_PARITY.md` and README wording so the operator-facing support claim matches the tested surface exactly.
- Ensure test output/logs emit machine-parseable scenario identifiers for success and failure cases.

Acceptance:
- There is deterministic coverage for mounting a named subvolume and a named snapshot via the public mount path.
- README and `FEATURE_PARITY.md` no longer overclaim relative to the tested operator surface.
- Coverage artifacts make it clear whether support is internal-only or operator-visible.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","docs","e2e","mount","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sv3d4","depends_on_id":"bd-sv1b2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-sv3d4","depends_on_id":"bd-sv2c3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-mexte","title":"add mounted-path E2E and log-contract coverage for ext4 EXT4_IOC_MOVE_EXT","description":"Survey finding: the repo now has FUSE-side `EXT4_IOC_MOVE_EXT` parsing/validation and an open backend bead (`bd-pjcz6`), but no operator-visible mounted-path proof that the ioctl actually succeeds and preserves data semantics once the backend is implemented.

Scope:
- Add real mounted-path coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` (or equivalent) for successful `EXT4_IOC_MOVE_EXT` execution after `bd-pjcz6` lands.
- Verify moved-length semantics, post-move file contents, donor/source extent state, and failure paths that should surface stable errno values.
- Assert structured logging fields for success and rejection cases (`operation_id`, `scenario_id`, `outcome`, and error class where applicable).

Acceptance:
- There is at least one mounted-path success case and one mounted-path rejection case for `EXT4_IOC_MOVE_EXT`.
- E2E assertions prove no silent data loss or incorrect moved-length reporting.
- Logging coverage makes ioctl outcomes auditable in CI and operator troubleshooting.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","ext4","fuse","ioctl","logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mexte","depends_on_id":"bd-pjcz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-mmplg","title":"add CLI and runtime log-contract coverage for ext4 MMP refusal states","description":"Survey finding: ext4 MMP read-side validation is implemented and there is already an open conformance bead (`bd-h4vn9`) for active-MMP write-open rejection, but the operator-facing surface still lacks explicit CLI/runtime log-contract coverage for fsck/active/unsafe MMP refusal paths.

Scope:
- Add focused tests that open or mount ext4 images with `MMP` in `Clean`, `Fsck`, `Active`, and `UnsafeUnknown` states.
- Verify user-visible error messages and errno mappings remain stable for write-participating flows.
- Assert structured tracing fields for refusal paths, including status class and sequence when present.
- Keep clean-state behavior unchanged.

Acceptance:
- The operator-facing mount/open path has deterministic coverage for each non-clean MMP state.
- Structured logs surface enough detail for incident/debug use without raw block dumps.
- The new coverage complements, rather than duplicates, the lower-level conformance bead.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","logging","mount","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mmplg","depends_on_id":"bd-h4vn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
